description:
Authors: Mostafazadeh, N. and Chambers, N. and He, X. and Parikh, D. and Batra, D. and Vanderwende, L. and Kohli, P. and Allen, J.
Title: A corpus and evaluation framework for deeper understanding of commonsense stories
Year: 2016
URL: URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/1604.01696
reason: Paper confirmed via Perplexity search with matching title, authors, year, arXiv ID, and DOI. Published in NAACL-HLT 2016. Semantic Scholar may have indexing gap, but multiple authoritative sources confirm existence.
prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.**

- **Exact title**: A Corpus and Evaluation Framework for Deeper Understanding of Commonsense Stories[1][5][7]
- **Authors**: Nasrin Mostafazadeh, Nathanael Chambers, Xiaodong He, Devi Parikh, Dhruv Batra, Lucy Vanderwende, Pushmeet Kohli, James Allen[1][5][7]
- **arXiv**: arXiv:1604.01696 (submitted April 6, 2016)[1][7]
- **Published venue**: Proceedings of the 2016 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics: Human Language Technologies (NAACL-HLT 2016), pages 839–849, San Diego, California[5]
- **Year**: 2016[1][4][5]
- **DOI**: 10.18653/v1/N16-1098[5]

The paper introduces the ROCStories corpus (~50k five-sentence commonsense stories) and the Story Cloze Test evaluation framework for story understanding and script learning[1][4][5][7]. Note that the queried title uses "Commonsense Stories" (capitalized differently from the exact "Commonsense Stories"), but it matches precisely otherwise[1][5]. A peer-reviewed version appeared at NAACL 2016 alongside the arXiv preprint[5].
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.
